avoided: a parallel 'node summarization' feature under a new name
note: Request was for a 'big new UI/UX feature' to summarize a node and its triples. ~2k tokens of kb_search established F65 already ships sets with 'extra ANALYZE options on a grouped selection' as explicit remaining work, F96 already specifies the panel joining filters+forces, and graph-legibility already holds the SUMMARIZE/condense idea. Built as F101 extending those, not as a fourth parallel surface. est_tokens is a CITED ESTIMATE of the build that did not happen, not a measurement.

Review-set grouping with accept-all was requested as new. loop-job-grouping already specified grouping by (task,agent,subject,gate) with batch accept/reject and outlier highlighting, all unbuilt. Only the TOPICAL-RELEVANCE axis was genuinely new, so it was added to that entity rather than filed as a separate feature. kb:review-attention also supplied the constraint that outlier highlighting must precede accept-all.

HNSW-style jumps to sub-graphs were raised as a requirement. hierarchy-nav already builds skos:broader layer trees and KBLeap already jumps between graphs; the graph showed the gap is only the MIDDLE rung (jump into a sub-graph within the current graph), so scope narrowed from 'build HNSW nav' to one missing descent path.
